note:
{
  "experiment_overview": "The experiment aims to fabricate white light-emitting electrochemical cells with stable emission from exciplexes. The goal is to develop a method for creating efficient and stable light-emitting devices.",
  "procedures_observed": [
    "A person in a laboratory setting prepares solutions using a balance and pipette, then places them into a centrifuge for mixing.",
    "The mixture is then subjected to an evaporation process using a vacuum chamber.",
    "Subsequently, the solution is coated onto a substrate using a spin coater.",
    "Finally, the coated substrate is exposed to UV light for curing.",
    "The experiment concludes with the measurement of light emission intensity using a spectrometer."
  ],
  "materials_and_subjects": [
    "Solutions containing organic compounds (e.g., 2-TNATA, DMFLNPB)",
    "Substrates (e.g., glass or plastic films)",
    "UV light source",
    "Spectrometer"
  ],
  "tools_and_setup": [
    "Balance",
    "Pipette",
    "Centrifuge",
    "Spin coater",
    "Vacuum chamber",
    "Spectrometer"
  ],
  "quantitative_observations": [
    "Volume of solutions mixed (e.g., 10 mL)",
    "Speed of centrifuge (e.g., 800 rpm for 60 seconds, 1,000 rpm over 3 seconds)",
    "Curing time under UV light (e.g., 6,000 mJ/cm² at 365 nm)",
    "Light emission intensity measured by spectrometer"
  ],
  "key_transitions": [
    "Mixing solutions in a centrifuge",
    "Evaporation of the mixture in a vacuum chamber",
    "Coating the substrate with the solution",
    "Exposure of the coated substrate to UV light",
